The Header.tsx looks fine - it has `flex items-center gap-3` on the container div. The issue might be with Tailwind CSS not being properly loaded or configured.

Looking at the screenshot, the layout is completely unstyled - text is just stacking vertically with no flex layout. This is a strong sign that Tailwind CSS is **not being applied at all**.

Common causes:

1. Tailwind CSS not imported in the main CSS/entry file
2. Tailwind config not including the correct content paths

3. CSS not being loaded at all

Let me check the main CSS file and Tailwind config to see what's going on.

1 import {

4   createRouter as createTanStackRouter,
5 } from '@tanstack/react-router';
6 import { routeTree } from './routeTree.gen';
7 
8 /**
9  * Vite `base` uses a trailing slash (`/` or `/base-skill/`). TanStack `basepath`
10  * is normalized without a trailing slash (except root `/`).
11  */
12 function viteBaseToRouterBasepath(viteBase: string): string {
13   const trimmed = viteBase.replace(/\/+$/, '');
14   return trimmed === '' ? '/' : trimmed;
15 }
16 
17 const basepath = viteBaseToRouterBasepath(import.meta.env.BASE_URL);
18 
19 function ssrMemoryInitialPath(): string {
20   return basepath === '/' ? '/en/' : `${basepath}/en/`;
21 }
22 
23 export function getRouter() {
24   const history =
25     'window' in globalThis
26       ? createBrowserHistory()
27       : createMemoryHistory({
28           initialEntries: [ssrMemoryInitialPath()],
29         });
30 
31   const router = createTanStackRouter({
32     routeTree,
33     history,
34     basepath,
35     scrollRestoration: true,
36     defaultPreload: 'intent',
37     defaultPreloadStaleTime: 0,
38   });
39 
40   return router;
41 }
42

46   }
47 }

```text
1 import {
2   createFileRoute,
3   useNavigate,
4   useParams,
5 } from '@tanstack/react-router';
6 import { useMemo } from 'react';
7 import type { GameLevel, GameSubject } from '@/games/registry';
8 import type { ValidatorAdapter } from '@tanstack/react-router';
9 import { GameGrid } from '@/components/GameGrid';
10 import { LevelRow } from '@/components/LevelRow';
11 import { useBookmarks } from '@/db/hooks/useBookmarks';
12 import { filterCatalog, paginateCatalog } from '@/games/catalog-utils';
13 import { GAME_CATALOG } from '@/games/registry';
14 
15 type CatalogSearchInput = {
16   search?: string;
17   level?: string;
18   subject?: string;
19   page?: number | string;
20 };
21 
22 type CatalogSearchOutput = {
23   search: string;
24   level: string;
25   subject: string;
26   page: number;
27 };
28 
29 const catalogSearchValidator: ValidatorAdapter<
30   CatalogSearchInput,
31   CatalogSearchOutput
32 > = {
33   types: {
34     input: {} as CatalogSearchInput,
35     output: {} as CatalogSearchOutput,
36   },
37   parse: (input: unknown) => {
38     const raw = (input ?? {}) as Record<string, unknown>;
39     const pageRaw =
40       typeof raw.page === 'number' && Number.isFinite(raw.page)
41         ? raw.page
42         : Number.parseInt(String(raw.page ?? '1'), 10) || 1;
43     return {
44       search: typeof raw.search === 'string' ? raw.search : '',
45       level: typeof raw.level === 'string' ? raw.level : '',
46       subject: typeof raw.subject === 'string' ? raw.subject : '',
47       page: Math.max(1, pageRaw),
48     };
49   },
50 };
51 
52 const PAGE_SIZE = 12;
53 
54 const HomeScreen = () => {
55   const { level, subject, search, page } = Route.useSearch();
56   const { locale } = useParams({ from: '/$locale' });
57   const navigate = useNavigate({ from: '/$locale/' });
58   const { bookmarkedIds, toggle } = useBookmarks();
59 
60   const filtered = useMemo(
61     () =>
62       filterCatalog(GAME_CATALOG, {
63         search,
64         level: level as GameLevel | '',
65         subject: subject as GameSubject | '',
66       }),
67     [search, level, subject],
68   );
69 
70   const {
71     items,
72     page: safePage,
73     totalPages,
74   } = useMemo(
75     () => paginateCatalog(filtered, page, PAGE_SIZE),
76     [filtered, page],
77   );
78 
79   const updateSearch = (
80     patch: Partial<{
81       level: string;
82       subject: string;
83       search: string;
84       page: number;
85     }>,
86   ) => {
87     void navigate({
88       search: (prev) => ({ ...prev, ...patch }),
89     });
90   };
91 
92   const handlePlay = (gameId: string) => {
93     void navigate({
94       to: '/$locale/game/$gameId',
95       params: { locale, gameId },
96     });
97   };
98 
99   return (
100     <div className="px-4 py-2">
101       <LevelRow
102         currentLevel={level as GameLevel | ''}
103         onLevelChange={(l) => updateSearch({ level: l, page: 1 })}
104       />
105       <div className="mt-4">
106         <GameGrid
107           entries={items}
108           bookmarkedIds={bookmarkedIds}
109           onBookmarkToggle={toggle}
110           onPlay={handlePlay}
111           page={safePage}
112           totalPages={totalPages}
113           onPageChange={(p) => updateSearch({ page: p })}
114         />
115       </div>
116     </div>
117   );
118 };
119 
120 export const Route = createFileRoute('/$locale/_app/')({
121   validateSearch: catalogSearchValidator,
122   component: HomeScreen,
123 });
124
```

</details></tool-use>

_**Agent (claude-sonnet-4-6 2026-04-01 11:10:55Z)**_

Found the likely issue. In [styles.css](worktrees/milestone-3-app-shell/src/styles.css#L2), the Tailwind import uses the `url()` syntax:

```css
@import url('tailwindcss');
```

Tailwind v4's `@tailwindcss/vite` plugin specifically recognizes `@import "tailwindcss"` (string form). The `url()` wrapper can cause the plugin to not process it as a module, meaning utility classes like `flex` never get generated — but plain CSS (your custom properties, body background, link colors) still work because those are just regular CSS.

**The fix** — in [styles.css](worktrees/milestone-3-app-shell/src/styles.css) change lines 2-5 from `url()` to string form:

```css
@import "tailwindcss";
@import "tw-animate-css";
@import "shadcn/tailwind.css";
@import "@fontsource-variable/geist";
```

You can verify this is the cause by opening DevTools → Elements → inspect the `<div class="flex items-center gap-3 ...">` and checking if `display: flex` appears in the computed styles. If it's missing, Tailwind isn't generating the `flex` utility.

_**Agent (claude-sonnet-4-6 2026-04-01 11:16:36Z)**_

Yes — line 7 says it all: **auto-generated by TanStack Router**. The file header also has `/* eslint-disable */` and `// @ts-nocheck` on lines 1-2 to suppress all linting and type-checking. That's why ESLint/Prettier don't complain — the file opts itself out entirely.

It gets regenerated every time TanStack Router's dev server detects route file changes, so any manual edits would be overwritten anyway.

Looking at the ESLint flat config documentation, it's clear now: plugins are scoped to the configuration object they're defined in, so if multiple config objects use rules from the same plugin, that plugin needs to be included in each one. This means I need to add all the necessary plugins to every local config object that references their rules.

Scanning through the four config objects, I can see that config object 1 uses `import` and `@typescript-eslint` plugins, config object 2 uses `@typescript-eslint`, config object 3 uses `@stylistic`, and config object 4 uses `import`. The cleanest approach is to import all the required plugins at the top and then add them to each config object that needs them.

I'm also noticing the `pnpm/json-enforce-catalog` rule is set to off, so even if the pnpm plugin isn't registered, it shouldn't cause a failure. But I should take a more targeted approach and just address the immediate plugin registration errors rather than overhauling the entire config structure.
</details></think>

//  @ts-check

import eslintComments from '@eslint-community/eslint-plugin-eslint-comments';
import stylisticPlugin from '@stylistic/eslint-plugin';
import { tanstackConfig } from '@tanstack/eslint-config';
import eslintConfigPrettier from 'eslint-config-prettier/flat';
import importPlugin from 'eslint-plugin-import-x';
import jsxA11y from 'eslint-plugin-jsx-a11y';
import reactPlugin from 'eslint-plugin-react';
import reactHooks from 'eslint-plugin-react-hooks';
import eslintPluginUnicorn from 'eslint-plugin-unicorn';
import tseslint from 'typescript-eslint';

// Note: eslint-plugin-tailwindcss v3 is incompatible with Tailwind CSS v4
// Tailwind v4 removed the `./resolveConfig` subpath export that this plugin requires.
// Re-enable once eslint-plugin-tailwindcss adds Tailwind v4 support.

export default [
  ...tanstackConfig,
  reactPlugin.configs.flat.recommended,
  reactPlugin.configs.flat['jsx-runtime'],
  // eslint-plugin-react-hooks v7 ships a flat config under configs.flat
  // but the top-level recommended-latest still uses the legacy string array format,
  // so we construct the flat config object manually here.
  {
    plugins: {
      'react-hooks': reactHooks,
      // Only `require-description`: unicorn has no equivalent for mandatory `-- <why>` on disables.
      'eslint-comments': eslintComments,
    },
    rules: reactHooks.configs['recommended-latest'].rules,
  },
  jsxA11y.flatConfigs.recommended,
  eslintPluginUnicorn.configs.recommended,
  // Global plugin registry — makes plugins available to all subsequent config objects.
  // ESLint v9 flat config requires plugins to be declared in each config object that
  // uses their rules; registering here (no rules) satisfies that requirement globally.
  {
    plugins: {
      import: importPlugin,
      '@stylistic': stylisticPlugin,
      '@typescript-eslint': tseslint.plugin,
    },
  },
  {
    settings: {
      react: { version: 'detect' },
    },
    rules: {
      // Unicorn `recommended` is on above; these rules clash with React (PascalCase files,
      // `props`), TanStack Router (`$param` routes), and routine TS/React naming (`db`, `doc`).
      // Do not rename/destructure around third-party typings (e.g. `React.ComponentProps`,
      // Radix); disable rules instead of "fixing" imports or prop shapes to satisfy ESLint.
      'unicorn/filename-case': 'off',
      'unicorn/prevent-abbreviations': 'off',
      'unicorn/no-null': 'off',
      // Prefer explicit `./` in `new URL(..., import.meta.url)` (unicorn default is `never`).
      'unicorn/relative-url-style': ['error', 'always'],

      // Applies to every `eslint-disable-*` / `eslint-enable` line, including `unicorn/…` rules.
      'eslint-comments/require-description': 'error',

      'import/no-default-export': 'error',
      'import/no-cycle': 'off',
      'pnpm/json-enforce-catalog': 'off',

      'import/order': [
        'error',
        {
          groups: [
            'builtin',
            'external',
            'internal',
            'parent',
            'sibling',
            'index',
            'object',
            'type',
          ],
          alphabetize: { order: 'asc', caseInsensitive: true },
        },
      ],

      // Layout (semicolons, quotes, commas, spacing, arrows) is handled by Prettier +
      // eslint-config-prettier. Trailing commas: `trailingComma: 'all'` in prettier.config.js.
      'arrow-body-style': ['error', 'as-needed'],

      'react/function-component-definition': [
        'error',
        {
          namedComponents: 'arrow-function',
          unnamedComponents: 'arrow-function',
        },
      ],
      'react/jsx-max-depth': ['warn', { max: 5 }],
      'react/no-unstable-nested-components': 'error',
      'react/no-array-index-key': 'warn',
      // Shadcn/Radix wrappers use `{...props}` with `ComponentProps<typeof Primitive>`;
      // enforcing no-spread would require per-file disables or unnatural APIs.
      'react/jsx-props-no-spreading': 'off',
      'react/no-unknown-property': 'error',
    },
  },
  // TypeScript-specific rules scoped to TS/TSX files — these require the TypeScript
  // parser (set up by tanstackConfig) and must not run on plain JS or config files.
  {
    files: ['**/*.{ts,tsx}'],
    rules: {
      '@typescript-eslint/array-type': 'off',
      '@typescript-eslint/require-await': 'off',
      '@typescript-eslint/no-explicit-any': 'error',
      '@typescript-eslint/no-unsafe-assignment': 'error',
      '@typescript-eslint/no-unused-vars': [
        'error',
        {
          argsIgnorePattern: '^_',
          varsIgnorePattern: '^_',
        },
      ],
    },
  },
  {
    files: ['src/**/*.ts', '!src/**/*.tsx'],
    rules: {
      '@typescript-eslint/explicit-module-boundary-types': 'warn',
    },
  },
  eslintConfigPrettier,
  // Prettier disables these in eslint-config-prettier; we re-enable after it.
  // For operator-linebreak, use `null` as the position so the rule's built-in default
  // applies: `?` / `:` -> "before" only; other operators use "after" (Prettier-friendly).
  // Passing `"before"` globally fights Prettier on `=`, `&&`, `&` in types, etc.
  {
    rules: {
      '@stylistic/multiline-ternary': ['error', 'always-multiline'],
      '@stylistic/operator-linebreak': [
        'error',
        null,
        {
          // Prettier often puts leading `|` / `&` in multiline union/intersection types.
          overrides: { '|': 'ignore', '&': 'ignore' },
        },
      ],
    },
  },
  {
    // Framework config files require `export default` by convention (Vite, Vitest,
    // Playwright, Knip); allow it only in these root-level config files.
    files: [
      'vite.config.ts',
      'vitest.config.ts',
      'playwright.config.ts',
      'knip.config.ts',
    ],
    rules: {
      'import/no-default-export': 'off',
    },
  },
  {
    // TanStack Router output; ships with undescribed blanket eslint-disable
    ignores: [
      'eslint.config.js',
      'prettier.config.js',
      '**/routeTree.gen.ts',
      '.specstory/**',
    ],
  },
];

<summary>Tool use: **Write**</summary>

```mjs
const skip = (files) => files.filter((f) => !f.includes('/.specstory/'));

const cmd = (command, files) => {
  const filtered = skip(files);
  return filtered.length > 0 ? [`${command} ${filtered.join(' ')}`] : [];
};

export default {
  '*.{ts,tsx}': (files) => [
    ...cmd('eslint --fix', files),
    ...cmd('prettier --write', files),
  ],
  '*.css': (files) => [
    ...cmd('stylelint --fix', files),
    ...cmd('prettier --write', files),
  ],
  '*.md': (files) => [
    ...cmd('markdownlint-cli2 --fix', files),
    ...cmd('prettier --write', files),
  ],
  '*.json': (files) => cmd('prettier --write', files),
};

```
